parts.answer: Indicative content guidance Answers must be credited by using the level descriptors (below) in line with the general marking guidance. The indicative content below exemplifies some of the points that candidates may make but this does not imply that any of these must be included. Other relevant points must also be credited. Quantitative skills assessed QS4: Construct and interpret a range of standard graphical forms QS9: Interpret, apply and analyse information in written, graphical, tabular and numerical forms. Knowledge, Application and Analysis (8 marks) – indicative content • Definition of ‘external benefits’ – where there are positive impacts on third parties/where MSB is greater than MPB • Diagram showing external benefits • Consumers only consider their private costs and their private benefits • Consumers ignore impacts on third parties/ external benefits • Consumers are undervaluing the benefit of education to society • The market equilibrium price, P ME is below the social optimum price, P SO • The market equilibrium quantity, Q ME is below the social optimum price, Q SO • Limited number of years of education just 4.4 years for adults • The welfare gain that can be achieved by moving to social optimum is shaded/ labelled • Under-consumption of education Q SO -Q ME, • Private benefits could include higher income over life time • External benefits could include lower government spending on health, welfare, and prisons; less poverty, better international competitiveness, new ideas and improved technology • Under-investment in education as wages can be earned in vanilla production • Benefits of employment may be greater than any lost benefit from under-consumption of education • 20 000 children aged 12 to 17 work in vanilla production/ 32% of workforce • Families need the child to work to supplement income • With rising costs of living children need to work so families can afford food • Poor conditions and overcrowding make education less attractive • Cyclones d
